title: It is always possible to represent a tree by a one-dimensional i
content: It is always possible to represent a tree by a one-dimensional integer array.  ~@[](1)
author: 徐镜春
description: It is always possible to represent a tree by a one-dimensional integer array.

title: There exists a binary tree with 2016 nodes in total, and with 16
content:
There exists a binary tree with 2016 nodes in total, and with 16 nodes having only one child.
~@[](3)
author: 何钦铭
description: There exists a binary tree with 2016 nodes in total, and with 16 nodes having only one child.

title: Given a tree of degree 3. Suppose that there are 3 nodes of degr
content:
Given a tree of degree 3. Suppose that there are 3 nodes of degree 2 and 2 nodes of degree 3. Then the number of leaf nodes must be \_\_\_\_.


@[](3)

A. 5
B. 6
C. 7
D. 8
author: 陈越
description: Given a tree of degree 3. Suppose that there are 3 nodes of degree 2 and 2 nodes of degree 3. Then the number of leaf nodes must be \_\_\_\_.

title: If a general tree $$T$$ is converted into a binary tree $$BT$$,
content:
If a general tree $$T$$ is converted into a binary tree $$BT$$, then which of the following $$BT$$ traversals gives the same sequence as that of the post-order traversal of $$T$$?  @[](2)

A. Pre-order traversal
B. In-order traversal
C. Post-order traversal
D. Level-order traversal
author: 考研真题
description: If a general tree $$T$$ is converted into a binary tree $$BT$$, then which of the following $$BT$$ traversals gives the same sequence as that of the post-order traversal of $$T$$?

title: Isomorphic
author: 陈越

title: ZigZagging on a Tree
content:
Suppose that all the keys in a binary tree are distinct positive integers.  A unique binary tree can be determined by a given pair of postorder and inorder traversal sequences.  And it is a simple standard routine to print the numbers in level-order.  However, if you think the problem is too simple, then you are too naive.  This time you are supposed to print the numbers in "zigzagging order" -- that is, starting from the root, print the numbers level-by-level, alternating between left to right and right to left.  For example, for the following tree you must output: 1 11 5 8 17 12 20 15.

![zigzag.jpg](~/337cbfb0-a7b2-4500-9664-318e9ffc870e.jpg)

### Input Specification:

Each input file contains one test case.  For each case, the first line gives a positive integer N ($$\le$$30), the total number of nodes in the binary tree.  The second line gives the inorder sequence and the third line gives the postorder sequence.  All the numbers in a line are separated by a space.

### Output Specification:

For each test case, print the zigzagging sequence of the tree in a line.  All the numbers in a line must be separated by exactly one space, and there must be no extra space at the end of the line.

### Sample Input:
```in
8
12 11 20 17 1 15 8 5
12 20 17 11 15 8 5 1
```

### Sample Output:
```out
1 11 5 8 17 12 20 15
```
author: 陈越

And it is a simple standard routine to print the numbers in level-order.  However, if you think the problem is too simple, then you are too naive.  This time you are supposed to print the numbers in "zigzagging order" -- that is, starting from the root, print the numbers level-by-level, alternating between left to right and right to left.  For example, for the following tree you must output: 1 11 5 8 17 12 20 15.

![zigzag.jpg](~/337cbfb0-a7b2-4500-9664-318e9ffc870e.jpg)

### Input Specification:

Each input file contains one test case.  For each case, the first line gives a positive integer N ($$\le$$30), the total number of nodes in the binary tree.  The second line gives the inorder sequence and the third line gives the postorder sequence.  All the numbers in a line are separated by a space.

### Output Specification:

For each test case, print the zigzagging sequence of the tree in a line.  All the numbers in a line must be separated by exactly one space, and there must be no extra space at the end of the line.

### Sample Input:
```in
8
12 11 20 17 1 15 8 5
12 20 17 11 15 8 5 1
```

### Sample Output:
```out
1 11 5 8 17 12 20 15
```

title: Tree Traversals Again
content:
An inorder binary tree traversal can be implemented in a non-recursive way with a stack. For example, suppose that when a 6-node binary tree (with the keys numbered from 1 to 6) is traversed, the stack operations are: push(1); push(2); push(3); pop(); pop(); push(4); pop(); pop(); push(5); push(6); pop(); pop(). Then a unique binary tree (shown in Figure 1) can be generated from this sequence of operations. Your task is to give the postorder traversal sequence of this tree.
![](~/30)
Figure 1
### Input Specification: Each input file contains one test case. For each case, the first line contains a positive integer $$N$$ ($$\le 30$$) which is the total number of nodes in a tree (and hence the nodes are numbered from 1 to $$N$$). Then $$2N$$ lines follow, each describes a stack operation in the format: "Push X" where X is the index of the node being pushed onto the stack; or "Pop" meaning to pop one node from the stack. ### Output Specification: For each test case, print the postorder traversal sequence of the corresponding tree in one line. A solution is guaranteed to exist. All the numbers must be separated by exactly one space, and there must be no extra space at the end of the line. ### Sample Input: ```in 6 Push 1 Push 2 Push 3 Pop Pop Push 4 Pop Pop Push 5 Push 6 Pop Pop ``` ### Sample Output: ```out 3 4 2 6 5 1 ```
author: 陈越
description:

For example, suppose that when a 6-node binary tree (with the keys numbered from 1 to 6) is traversed, the stack operations are: push(1); push(2); push(3); pop(); pop(); push(4); pop(); pop(); push(5); push(6); pop(); pop(). Then a unique binary tree (shown in Figure 1) can be generated from this sequence of operations. Your task is to give the postorder traversal sequence of this tree.
![](~/30)
Figure 1
### Input Specification: Each input file contains one test case. For each case, the first line contains a positive integer $$N$$ ($$\le 30$$) which is the total number of nodes in a tree (and hence the nodes are numbered from 1 to $$N$$). Then $$2N$$ lines follow, each describes a stack operation in the format: "Push X" where X is the index of the node being pushed onto the stack; or "Pop" meaning to pop one node from the stack. ### Output Specification: For each test case, print the postorder traversal sequence of the corresponding tree in one line. A solution is guaranteed to exist. All the numbers must be separated by exactly one space, and there must be no extra space at the end of the line. ### Sample Input: ```in 6 Push 1 Push 2 Push 3 Pop Pop Push 4 Pop Pop Push 5 Push 6 Pop Pop ``` ### Sample Output: ```out 3 4 2 6 5 1 ```
